response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is primarily a web and full-stack developer whose career has centered on TypeScript, Node.js, React, and web platforms. C++ experience is limited to a six-month role at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing components for a C IDE and a bachelor's thesis project; C++ does not appear in the resume's skills section at all. The role demands deep, production-grade C++ expertise, multi-threaded systems programming, industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), and IEC 62443 security standards — none of which are demonstrated in the candidate's background. While the candidate shows strong agile discipline, event-driven architecture experience (in DLang and Node.js), and high-performance systems work at Sociomantic, these do not compensate for the fundamental mismatch with the core technical requirements.
strengths: Demonstrated event-driven and distributed systems thinking at scale (Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second, GISCollective platform)
Strong agile/TDD discipline with CI/CD, trunk-based development, and a consistent quality mindset across multiple senior roles
Broad systems exposure including low-latency programming (DLang async I/O, fibers), embedded work (robotic arms at Altom), and GIS/geospatial platforms
concerns: C++ is effectively absent from the candidate's active skill set — only 6 months of C/C++ at NI and a thesis project, with no listing in the skills summary
No exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443, MQTT, or any industrial protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0) central to the role
No Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) or cybersecurity practice mentioned; cloud experience is AWS-based rather than Azure
